cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user wants to know what the 1974 World Bank study concluded about economic growth in some developing countries. First, I need to recall the passage. The key part is where it says, "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, the wording here is a bit tricky. It says "little benefit to a third," which means that a third of the population got little benefit, implying that the rest might have benefited more. But the options are about what the growth brought overall. Looking at the options: (A) benefit to a third, (B) benefit to two-thirds, (C) little benefit to their people, (D) no benefit at all. The passage states that the growth was of "little benefit to a third of their population," so that suggests that the majority (two-thirds) might have had some benefit.
